name: report_ucf_timing
full_help:
report_ucf_timing

Description: 
Report timing paths

Syntax: 
report_ucf_timing  [-file <arg>] [-append] [-name <arg>] [-return_string]
                   [-speed <arg>] [-from <args>] [-to <args>] [-thru <args>]
                   [-min_max <arg>] [-transition <arg>] [-sort_by <arg>]
                   [-interconnect_type <arg>] [-maxpaths <arg>] [-nworst <arg>]
                   [-required_times] [-quiet] [-verbose]

Usage: 
  Name                  Description
  ---------------------------------
  [-file]               Filename to output results to. (send output to 
                        console if -file is not used)
  [-append]             Append the results to file, don't overwrite the 
                        results file
  [-name]               Output the results to GUI panel with this name
                        Default: results_1
  [-return_string]      return report as string
  [-speed]              Speed grade name
  [-from]               Filter by starting pin or instance name
  [-to]                 Filter by ending pin or instance name
  [-thru]               Filter by intermediate pin or instance names
  [-min_max]            Run max (setup) or min (hold) timing analysis
  [-transition]         Report rise or fall transitions
  [-sort_by]            Sort criterion
  [-interconnect_type]  Interconnect delay type
  [-maxpaths]           Limit the report to this many paths
                        Default: 1
  [-nworst]             Limit the report to this many paths per endpoint
                        Default: 1
  [-required_times]     Calculate all required times
  [-quiet]              Ignore command errors
  [-verbose]            Suspend message limits during command execution

Categories: 
Report
